zh.lifeSummary: 當Henry Charles Foxe "Vaux"於1490年出生在英國藍卡爾時，他的父親John Davis Fox27歲，母親Elizabeth Hannah25歲。他與Anne Woodward Hawes結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。他去世於1587年在他的家鄉享年97歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭米德萊可。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Henry Charles Foxe "Vaux"于1490年出生在英国藍卡爾时，他的父亲John Davis Fox27岁，母亲Elizabeth Hannah25岁。他与Anne Woodward Hawes结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。他去世于1587年在他的家乡享年97岁，埋葬在英国英格兰米德萊可。

nameMeanings.name: Foxe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Fox
meanings: English: nickname from a word denoting the animal (Middle English, Old English fox), widely used to denote a sly or cunning individual. It was also used for someone with red hair. In England this surname absorbed some early examples of surnames derived from the ancient Germanic personal names mentioned at Faulks and Foulks .
Irish: part translation of Gaelic Mac an tSionnaigh ‘son of the fox’ (see Tinney ).
Irish: also adopted for Ó Catharnaigh, see Kearney .

nameMeanings.name: "Vaux"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of various places in northern France called Vaux, from the Old French plural of val ‘valley’. Compare English Vause and French Devaux .

History: Richard Vaux (1749–90) emigrated from Sussex, England, to Philadelphia, PA, in early youth. Calvert Vaux, landscape architect, emigrated from London, England, to America in 1850 and designed several major US parks, including Central Park in New York City (with Frederick Olmstead). — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (along with its variant Veaux and altered form Devoe ) and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Vause
meanings: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Vaux (see Vaux ), a common French placename, singular and plural of val ‘valley’, the plural form being Latinized in surnames as de Vallibus. Norman lords with this surname are recorded in 12th- and early 13th-century records in Cumberland (Irthington), Devon (Farwood, in Colyton), East Yorkshire (Warter), Essex (Belchamp), and Leicestershire (Belvoir). Compare Vale .

nickname perhaps from Middle English fals, faus, vaus (Old French fals, faus, Old English fals) ‘deceitful, disloyal, dishonest, mendacious’, but it is more likely the name arises from 1, with the common Anglo-Norman French substitution of le for de in their surname.

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

nameMeanings.name: Charles
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Germanic word, karl, meaning ‘free man’, akin to Old English ceorl ‘man’. The name, Latin form Carolus, owed its popularity in medieval Europe to the Frankish leader Charlemagne ( ?742–814 ), who in 800 established himself as Holy Roman Emperor. His name (Latin Carolus Magnus) means ‘Charles the Great’. Carolus—or Karl, the German form—was a common name among Frankish leaders, including Charlemagne's grandfather Charles Martel ( 688–741 ). Charles is the French form. The name occurs occasionally in medieval Britain as Karolus or Carolus; it had a certain vogue in West Yorkshire from the 1400s, particularly among gentry families. The form Charles was chosen by Mary Queen of Scots ( 1542–87 ), who had been brought up in France, for her son, Charles James ( 1566–1625 ), who became King James VI of Scotland and, from 1603 , James I of England. His son and grandson both reigned as King Charles , and the name thus became established in the 17th century both in the Stuart royal house and among English and Scottish supporters of the Stuart monarchy. In the 18th century it was to some extent favoured, along with James , by Jacobites, supporters of the exiled Stuarts, opposed to the Hanoverian monarchy, especially in the Highlands of Scotland. In the 19th century the popularity of the name was further enhanced by romanticization of the story of ‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’, leader of the 1745 rebellion.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Henry Charles Foxe "Vaux" was born in 1490, in Lancashire, England, his father, John Davis Fox, was 27 and his mother, Elizabeth Hannah, was 25. He married Anne Woodward Hawes. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. He died in 1587, in his hometown, at the age of 97, and was buried in Middlesex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Henry Charles Foxe "Vaux" est né en 1490, à Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, John Davis Fox, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Hannah, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Anne Woodward Hawes. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Il est décédé en 1587, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 97 ans, et a été enterré à Middlesex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Henry Charles Foxe "Vaux" 1490 in Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Davis Fox 27 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Hannah 25. Er hat Anne Woodward Hawes geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Er starb 1587 im Alter von 97 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Middlesex, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Henry Charles Foxe "Vaux" è nato il nel 1490 nel Lancashire, England, il suo padre, John Davis Fox, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Hannah, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Anne Woodward Hawes. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì nel 1587, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 97 anni, e fu sepolto a Middlesex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Henry Charles Foxe "Vaux"が1490年にイギリスランカシュアイアで生まれた時，父John Davis Foxは27歳，母Elizabeth Hannahは25歳であった。Anne Woodward Hawesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼は1587年に故郷で97歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスミッドレセックスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Henry Charles Foxe "Vaux"(이)가 1490년 영국 랭카실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Davis Fox(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Hannah(은)는 25세였다. 그는 Anne Woodward Hawes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1587년 그의 고향에서 97세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Henry Charles Foxe "Vaux" nasceu em 1490, em Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Davis Fox, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Hannah, tinha 25 anos. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Henry Charles Foxe "Vaux" родился в 1490 г. в местности ланкашир, Англия, его отцу по имени John Davis Fox было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Hannah, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Anne Woodward Hawes. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Он умер в 1587 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 97 лет и был похоронен в местности миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Henry Charles Foxe "Vaux" nació en 1490, en Lancashire, Inglaterra, su papá, John Davis Fox, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Hannah, tenía 25 años.
